question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the StreamFileListener interface does not define whether onEnd() must be called exactly once per file, causing a listener that maintains a counter (e.g., executed AtomicBoolean in migration listeners) to be bypassed if onEnd() is called zero times due to a parsing skip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file that causes onEnd() to receive a StreamFile<?> with a consensusEnd that is less than the consensusEnd of the previously processed file, causing out-of-order data to be inserted into the database and breaking time-ordered queries?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file that causes onEnd() to be invoked with a StreamFile<?> whose getCount() returns a value that does not match the actual number of items in getItems(), causing inconsistent record counts to be stored in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the StreamFileListener interface does not define behavior when onEnd() is called with a StreamFile<?> that has already been cleared (via clear()), causing listener implementations to persist empty or default-value records to the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file that causes onEnd() to receive a StreamFile<?> with a null nodeAccountId, causing a NullPointerException in listener implementations that use the node account ID for entity resolution?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file that causes onEnd() to be called with a StreamFile<?> whose hash is a valid hex string of incorrect length (e.g., 32 bytes instead of 48 for SHA-384), causing silent hash comparison failures that allow forged files to pass chain verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the StreamFileListener interface is generic and type-erased at runtime, causing a raw-type listener registration to accept a StreamFile<?> of the wrong subtype and trigger a ClassCastException deep inside a listener implementation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file that causes onEnd() to receive a StreamFile<?> with a sidecars list of extremely large size, causing onEnd() to perform unbounded database inserts and exhaust database connection resources?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file that causes onEnd() to be invoked with a StreamFile<?> whose previousHash is set to the hash of a future (not yet processed) file, causing the hash chain to be accepted out of order and allowing replay of old stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that onEnd() throws ImporterException (a checked exception) but the composite listener does not catch it, causing a single failing listener to abort the entire composite dispatch and leave other listeners in an uncalled state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file that causes onEnd() to receive a StreamFile<?> with a loadEnd timestamp of 0, causing the totalDurationMetric to record a large negative duration and triggering false alerts in monitoring systems?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file that causes onEnd() to be called with a StreamFile<?> whose name matches an already-processed file name, causing a unique constraint violation in the database that halts the parsing pipeline and prevents processing of all subsequent files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the StreamFileListener interface does not define whether onEnd() is called within or outside a transaction, causing a listener that assumes transactional context to perform non-atomic operations when called outside a transaction boundary?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file that causes onEnd() to receive a StreamFile<?> with a null type field, causing a NullPointerException in AbstractStreamFileParser.shouldParse() when type.isChained() is called, crashing the parser?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file that causes onEnd() to be invoked with a StreamFile<?> whose items list contains duplicate consensus timestamps, causing unique constraint violations in the database when listener implementations attempt to persist all items?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the StreamFileListener interface does not define cleanup semantics, causing a listener that allocates resources in onEnd() to leak those resources if an ImporterException is thrown partway through processing?
